text: Jeff was standing outside the school, staring at the shuttered door. He looked like he hadn't slept.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: Abel. I was hoping you'd come by.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: I've been thinking. About the school. About a lot of things.
Jeff's gaze drifted toward the sea.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: Do you know why there are no children in the school, Abel?

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: I... assumed they were in class when I wasn't looking.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: There are no children. There haven't been for a very long time.
His voice cracked on the last word.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: This town... it doesn't change, Abel. It hasn't changed in seventy years. The same people, the same faces, the same... everything.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: I'm saying... you don't have to go back out there. Walsh will tell you it's just another fishing trip. It isn't.
Jeff looked over his shoulder, as if checking whether anyone was listening.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: They bring people in from the outside. Always have. 'Fresh blood,' they call it. The apprenticeship, the hospitality — it's all...
He stopped himself. His hands were shaking.
